firmware.summary: Beta 8 is a substantial update over Beta 7, adding new device support, much more personalisation and several important fixes.
firmware.changes:
ChargeScreen Beta 8.26

Added experimental support for Daly BMS batteries, including Foxygen batteries.

Added experimental support for Renogy Bluetooth batteries.

Rear BOOT button controls:

Short press: next dashboard screen.

Hold for two seconds: Wi‑Fi hotspot on/off.

Improved Wi‑Fi setup-page usability, including nearer Save Settings controls and moving Clear Saved Keys beside device/key setup.

Updated BLE capture instructions to request only captures for unsupported devices, with the useful model and live-reading information needed for compatibility work.

Notes

Daly/Foxygen and Renogy support are experimental and need confirmation against more real batteries.








ChargeScreen Beta 8

Firmware 8.18 adds to fixes for Fogstar batteries (4% issue).




Beta 8 is a substantial update over Beta 7, adding new device support, much more personalisation and several important fixes.

New device support

    ABC-BMS battery family, including compatible ABC, SOK, NB and Hoover app devices
    Victron Phoenix Inverter monitoring, showing power, battery voltage, AC output, operating state and alarms

More personalisation

    Give each screen or connected device its own custom name
    Customise the colours of individual screens
    Multiple devices of the same type can have their own names, colours and settings

Improved alarm handling

    Victron alarms are now shown visually on ChargeScreen
    When an alarm is detected, ChargeScreen automatically takes you to the affected device’s screen
    Alarm information is displayed clearly so it is easier to see which device needs attention

Battery improvements and bug fixes

    Fixed several problems when using multiple Bluetooth batteries
    Fogstar state-of-charge now follows the percentage reported by the battery, preventing incorrect readings above 100%
    Improved Roamer compatibility
    Improved battery discovery, selection and connection handling

The alarm feature is still being tested, so ChargeScreen should not be used as the only warning or safety system. Always continue to rely on the protection and alarm features built into your equipment.

Thank you to everyone who has tested the firmware, reported problems and supplied Bluetooth captures.
Those reports have been a huge help in improving Beta 8.

instructions.power: Power ChargeScreen using USB-C, or if you bought a rear power supply cable, carefully plug it in and make sure the notch on the cable lines up with the gap on the plug.
instructions.victron_intro: ChargeScreen reads Victron Instant Readout Bluetooth adverts. Those adverts are encrypted, so each Victron device needs its own Instant Readout encryption data.
instructions.victron_first_step: Open the VictronConnect app near your Victron device.
instructions.ble_intro: BLE capture records nearby Bluetooth Low Energy adverts for 5 minutes. It is useful when adding support for batteries or accessories that do not decode correctly yet.
instructions.ble_privacy: Privacy note: the CSV may include packets from nearby watches, phones, TVs, sensors, and batteries. Most of it is encrypted or only useful to the original device, but it will still appear in the file. Battery encryption codes are only used to decode the battery packets in the capture.

purchase.text:
ChargeScreen units are available premade and supplied with the firmware already installed. The device is £40, with postage added at checkout.

Postage to rest of the world is fine, but fees and custom charges may be possible, these can really add up.
